title: Objective-C Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Objective-C source files contain implementation code in Objective-C, a dynamically dispatched superset of C that was Apple's primary application programming language before Swift. It remains in use in legacy Apple platform codebases and frameworks.

## When to use
Use [Objective-C Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:objc;textable;code) for `.m` implementation files. Header files are covered by [C Header](https://capdag.com/browse/media:c-header;textable;code) since Objective-C headers use the `.h` extension. Files that mix Objective-C and C++ use the `.mm` extension and are handled by [Objective-C++ Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:objcpp;textable;code).

## Notes
Objective-C uses a Smalltalk-inspired message-passing syntax (`[receiver message]`) alongside standard C syntax. It supports both manual reference counting (MRC) and Automatic Reference Counting (ARC); the memory management model affects how code must be analyzed. Most modern Apple development uses [Swift Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:swift;textable;code), but large Objective-C codebases remain common in mature iOS and macOS applications.
